description: Provider-agnostic multi-agent project management: skills for Claude and Gemini + PTY overlay shell

tasks.title: Orchestrate skill — ledger-anchored rate cap derivation
tasks.description: Update the rate calibration logic in commands/orchestrate.md. Replace single-session delta method (cap = session_tokens / delta_pct) with ledger-anchored method: at session end, read agentflow_ledger.json, filter sessions where start_time >= window_start (local time; datetime.now() no tz), sum billable tokens (uncached_input + cache_creation + output per session), then cap_5hr = total_5hr_tokens / (end_pct_5hr / 100) and cap_wkly = total_wkly_tokens / (end_pct_wkly / 100). Window starts: 5hr = reset_time_5hr - 5h; weekly = reset_time_wkly - 7d. Gap: current session tokens not yet flushed to ledger — correct by adding (end_pct - start_pct) * prior_cap_estimate as current-session delta if ledger read returns fewer tokens than expected. Write updated cap estimates to ~/.agentflow/rate_calibration.json alongside existing EWMA fields.

unit: commands/orchestrate.md instructs ledger read at session end
commands/orchestrate.md filters sessions by start_time >= window_start (local time)
commands/orchestrate.md sums uncached_input + cache_creation + output per session
commands/orchestrate.md derives cap_5hr = total_5hr_tokens / (end_pct_5hr / 100)
commands/orchestrate.md derives cap_wkly = total_wkly_tokens / (end_pct_wkly / 100)
commands/orchestrate.md documents current-session gap and correction

tasks.acceptance_criteria: orchestrate.md rate calibration write uses ledger-anchored cap derivation; window start computation documented; current-session gap correction present

tasks.title: Oracle skill — general .idx reading protocol
tasks.description: Add a general Targeted Reads Rule section to commands/oracle.md. Currently oracle only checks .idx for architecture.md (Step 2a). All other file reads (lazy-loaded phase files: market.md 63L, checklist.md 113L, generation.md 133L; any future reads) have no .idx protocol. Add a 'Targeted Reads Rule' section matching the structure in commands/worker/system.md lines 51-78: compute HASH, check IDX path, grep for symbol, Read with offset+limit, fallback to full read if absent. Note: oracle's phase files (market.md, checklist.md, generation.md) are intentionally read in full when entering each phase — the rule applies to any targeted symbol/section lookup oracle makes outside of those full-phase loads.

unit: commands/oracle.md contains a Targeted Reads Rule section
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule instructs HASH computation
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule instructs grep for symbol in .idx
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule instructs Read with offset+limit
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule has fallback to full read when .idx absent

tasks.acceptance_criteria: oracle.md contains a general Targeted Reads Rule matching the worker protocol; existing Step 2a architecture.md-specific section preserved

tasks.title: PTY shell — local tiktoken tokenizer
tasks.description: Implement agentflow/shell/tokenizer.py. Single public function count(text: str) -> int using tiktoken cl100k_base encoding. Cache encoder at module level. No config reads, no LLM calls — pure function. Also create agentflow/shell/__init__.py (empty).

unit: count() returns correct token count for known strings
count() returns 0 for empty string
count() handles unicode and multi-line code snippets

security_constraints: No network calls — tiktoken must run fully offline
tasks.acceptance_criteria: count(text) returns tiktoken cl100k_base token count for any string input without network access

tasks.title: PTY shell — I/O interception wrapper
tasks.description: Implement agentflow/shell/pty_wrapper.py. Fork child process under a PTY using pty.fork(). I/O loop: relay bytes between parent stdout/stdin and child PTY fd using os.read/os.write + select.select. On each stdout chunk call tokenizer.count(chunk) and accumulate into self.accumulated_tokens. Handle SIGWINCH to resize PTY. Expose: PTYWrapper(command: list[str]); start() -> None; stop() -> None; accumulated_tokens: int property. Raise PTYError(message) on fork failure. stdlib-only: pty, os, select, signal, fcntl.

unit: accumulated_tokens increments after stdout chunk processed
PTYError raised when pty.fork fails
stop() sends SIGTERM to child and closes fd
integration: wrap echo command — verify stdout relayed and accumulated_tokens > 0

security_constraints: No shell=True — command must be list[str]
No LLM calls from this module
tasks.acceptance_criteria: PTYWrapper wraps a subprocess bidirectionally, relays I/O to terminal, and exposes accurate accumulated token count

tasks.title: PTY session manager — handoff, countdown, idx injection
tasks.description:
Implement agentflow/shell/session_manager.py. Wraps PTYWrapper with session logic. Config: read ~/.agentflow/config.toml via tomllib (Python 3.11+); fall back to hardcoded defaults if absent. Config keys read: shell.oracle_threshold_tokens (default 60000), shell.orchestrator_threshold_tokens (default 30000), shell.threshold_pct (default 0.30), shell.restart_delay_seconds (default 5). Responsibilities: (1) session_type detection — scan stdin for first /oracle or /orchestrate token; set session_type accordingly; (2) turn counter — increment on each assistant response boundary (detect by HANDOFF_RECOMMENDED or double-newline heuristic); (3) idx reminder injection — every 3 turns, write single-line idx banner to child stdin before next user input: '[IDX] Before next Read: check ~/.agentflow/cache/<hash>/index/<file>.idx — grep name:start-end, then Read(offset, limit).'; hash pre-computed via hashlib.sha256(cwd) at startup; (4) threshold check — after each turn check accumulated_tokens > session_threshold OR accumulated_tokens > window_size * threshold_pct; if either fires trigger handoff flow; (5) handoff flow — write '/handoff
' to stdin, scan stdout for HANDOFF_COMPLETE (timeout 120s), write '/clear
', countdown restart_delay_seconds with per-second stderr output (Ctrl+C cancels and exits cleanly), write session command ('/oracle
' or '/orchestrate
'); (6) manual passthrough — if /handoff detected in user stdin before threshold, suppress auto-restart after handoff completes. Entrypoint: run(command: list[str]) -> None.

unit: session_type set to 'oracle' when /oracle seen in stdin stream
session_type set to 'orchestrate' when /orchestrate seen in stdin stream
session_type remains None when no skill command seen
idx banner written to stdin every 3 turns
threshold check fires when accumulated_tokens exceeds oracle_threshold_tokens
threshold check fires when accumulated_tokens exceeds window_size * threshold_pct
manual /handoff in stdin suppresses auto-restart
countdown prints per-second output and can be interrupted with KeyboardInterrupt
integration: run() wraps a no-op subprocess and exits cleanly

security_constraints: config.toml path restricted to ~/.agentflow/ — validate before open
tasks.acceptance_criteria: session_manager.run() wraps a CLI process, detects session type, injects idx banner every 3 turns, and executes full handoff+restart flow when token threshold is exceeded
